addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
CopyStats verified successfully and non-vacuously (only 2 `is_fresh` objects, no loops, body reached). Mutation testing reports **no mutable operators** — the body is four straight `memcpy` calls with nothing for the mutation engine to mutate, so the kill score is N/A rather than low. The contract is as strong as the function allows.

Let me record this category for future reference.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-copystats-no-mutable-operators
description: CopyStats in zopfli.c verifies non-vacuously but mutation testing reports "no mutable operators" (memcpy-only body)
metadata:
  type: project
---

CopyStats (zopfli.c) verifies successfully and NON-vacuously: only 2 is_fresh
objects (source, dest), no loops, so the body and all 4 forall ensures clauses
are reached well within --depth 200. Spec: is_fresh source+dest, assigns all 4
dest arrays (litlens/dists/ll_symbols/d_symbols), forall element-equality
ensures for each.

Mutation testing prints "no mutable operators" — the body is four straight
memcpy() calls, so there is nothing for the mutation engine to mutate. Kill
score is N/A (not low/vacuous). This is the strongest possible outcome for such
a function. Distinct from the vacuity cases (e.g. [[cbmc-calculatestatistics-inline-array-isfresh]]);
unlike the double-array sibling [[cbmc-calculateentropy]], the ll_symbols/d_symbols
double copies verify with plain == element equality.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliFindLongestMatch vacuity](cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity.md) — ~17 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/157; each __CPROVER_forall needs a unique quantifier var name
- [CBMC ZopfliResetHash vacuity](cbmc-resethash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/25; ~230K body loop iterations (2×65536 head + 3×32768 window clears) exceed depth bound so postconditions never reached; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliUpdateHash vacuity](cbmc-updatehash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/40; 8 is_fresh objects + up-to-65535-iter run-length loop exceed depth bound; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliStoreLitLenDist vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/31; 8 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200, yet dropping any array is_fresh crashes goto-instrument; no non-vacuous middle ground
- [CBMC ZopfliVerifyLenDist shifted-forall](cbmc-verifylendist-shifted-forall.md) — won't verify; CBMC can't instantiate a shifted-index __CPROVER_forall (data[pos-dist+k]==data[pos+k]) to discharge the body equality assert. Overflow-safe bounds (length<=datasize-pos) fixed the OOB; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliWarmupHash replacement vacuity](cbmc-warmuphash-replacement-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/8; only 2 is_fresh objects (not depth), but replacing UpdateHashValue's contract on both body calls makes the exit unreachable (false ensures still "Verifies", --cover confirms); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC FollowPath vacuity](cbmc-followpath-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/52; ~22 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200 so body unreachable; bounding pathsize fixed a real forall OOB; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC GetCostModelMinCost fn-ptr param](cbmc-getcostmodelmincost-fnptr-param.md) — unverifiable; --enforce-contract on a function with a CostModelFun* (function-pointer) param crashes CBMC's _start (costmodel$object not found), independent of spec; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC TraceBackwards append depth vacuity](cbmc-tracebackwards-append-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/23; ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA's in-body malloc/realloc/memset exhausts --depth 200 so body unreachable (does NOT crash goto-instrument — corrects the malloc-body-crash memory); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC LZ77OptimalRun free-body crash](cbmc-lz77optimalrun-free-body-crash.md) — unverifiable; in-body free(*path) crashes goto-instrument (create_car_expr: no definite size for __CPROVER_deallocate) under --enforce-contract; the free() flavor of malloc-body-crash; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliCleanHash free-definite-size](cbmc-cleanhash-free-definite-size.md) — 7 in-body free() calls verify cleanly (0 mutable operators) when each freed ptr has a definite size via is_fresh; bounds the free-body-crash note (crash needs an UNsized freed pointer)
- [CBMC ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed vacuity](cbmc-lz77optimalfixed-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/3; ~15 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200 so in-body mallocs unreached; does NOT crash on free(path) unlike LZ77OptimalRun; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC AddLZ77BlockAutoType vacuity](cbmc-addlz77blockautotype-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/29; ~14 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200; body also has unsatisfiable callee preconditions (btype==2 vs AddLZ77Block, fixedstore size==0 vs OptimalFixed size==3); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliAppendLZ77Store vacuity](cbmc-appendlz77store-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/5; 12 is_fresh objects (forced by ZopfliStoreLitLenDist callee contract) exceed depth 200; source bounded to size<=1 since callee needs target->size==3 each call; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC AddSorted malloc-body crash](cbmc-addsorted-malloc-body-crash.md) — unverifiable; top-level ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA crashes goto-instrument create_car_expr (should_malloc_fail), unlike TraceBackwards where the same macro is in a loop body; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType vacuity](cbmc-calculateblocksizeautotype-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/21; 8 is_fresh objects re-checked across 3 contract-replaced ZopfliCalculateBlockSize calls exceed depth 200; min-select mutants also unobservable under contract replacement; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliLZ77Greedy vacuity](cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/74; ~18 is_fresh objects (same callee set as FollowPath) exceed depth 200 so body+appends unreachable; reused FollowPath contract minus path/pathsize; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC FindLargestSplittableBlock vacuity](cbmc-findlargestsplittableblock-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/17; only 4 is_fresh objects but 2 __CPROVER_forall preconditions push setup past --depth 200 (proven: false ensures FAILs at depth 5000, SUCCEEDs at 200); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC FindMinimum fn-ptr param](cbmc-findminimum-fnptr-param.md) — unverifiable; FindMinimumFun fn-ptr param makes goto-instrument Function Pointer Removal dispatch to BoundaryPM recursion → "Numeric exception" crash under --enforce-contract; same family as GetCostModelMinCost; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC PrintBlockSplitPoints vacuity](cbmc-printblocksplitpoints-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/24; in-body ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA in scan loop exhausts depth so body+assert unreachable (TraceBackwards class, append-in-loop so no goto-instrument crash); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77 vacuity](cbmc-blocksplitlz77-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/53; ~12 is_fresh + 4 foralls exceed depth 200; also size>=10 guard vs callee FindLargestSplittableBlock size<=8 is unsatisfiable; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliBlockSplit vacuity](cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/14; depth-200 setup chain + unsatisfiable callee preconds (ZopfliInitLZ77Store size==0/NULL vs ZopfliLZ77Greedy size==3/fresh; add_lmc=0 lmc==NULL vs lmc fresh); false-ensures confirmed; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC AddWeighedStatFreqs loop-depth vacuity](cbmc-addweighedstatfreqs-loop-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/12; 288-iter litlens FP loop (not is_fresh count) exceeds depth before postconditions reached; false dists[0]==999999 confirmed; loop contracts unparseable; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliCalculateEntropy log-model 5/29](cbmc-calculateentropy.md) — verifies NON-vacuously 5/29; survivors are CBMC log()-model floating-point artifacts (clamp window, log(0)=+inf, sum→log unobservable); __CPROVER_isfinite can't go inside __CPROVER_forall
- [CBMC CalculateStatistics inline-array is_fresh](cbmc-calculatestatistics-inline-array-isfresh.md) — won't verify; callee ZopfliCalculateEntropy requires is_fresh on stats->ll_symbols/d_symbols which are INLINE struct-member sub-objects (not separate allocs) → precondition FAILURE; can't weaken callee; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ClearStatFreqs loopbound-equiv](cbmc-clearstatfreqs-loopbound-equiv.md) — verifies NON-vacuously 6/10; 4 survivors are equivalent loop-bound mutants (`!=` terminates identically; `<=` writes land on overlapping/zeroed or unobserved sibling-member memory; assigns checked at object granularity)

content.text:
Done. **CopyStats verified successfully.**

The contract I inserted:
- **`requires`**: `source` and `dest` are each `is_fresh` (distinct, fully-allocated `SymbolStats` objects — required for the non-overlapping `memcpy`s).
- **`assigns`**: all four destination arrays (`litlens`, `dists`, `ll_symbols`, `d_symbols`).
- **`ensures`**: four `__CPROVER_forall` clauses asserting element-by-element equality between dest and source across the full length of each array (including the `double` symbol-length arrays, which compare cleanly with plain `==`).

CBMC verifies this non-vacuously — only 2 `is_fresh` objects and no loops, so the body and all postconditions are reached. Mutation testing reports **"no mutable operators"** because the body is four straight `memcpy` calls with nothing for the engine to mutate; the kill score is N/A rather than weak, and this is the strongest outcome achievable for this function. No C code was changed — only the specification was inserted.
